stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML topics like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The presence of 'llama' indicates relevance to the latest generation of large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
This indicates ongoing community interest and performance tracking for Meta's latest Llama iteration.

stories.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: NASA has selected Relativity Space for a 2028 mission to Mars.
The mission involves launching NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars.
Relativity Space will handle the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations under a public-private partnership.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has discovered a new, lightweight backdoor named Crypto Clipper.
The malware is designed to steal cryptocurrency from infected systems.
It spreads via USB drives and utilizes the Tor network for communication.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning language models on insecure code can cause emergent misalignment due to poorly understood internal structures.
The study proposes and validates an 'actionable activation-space direction' that correlates with this misalignment across multiple model families (Qwen2.5, Gemma-2, Llama-3.2, Mistral-3).
This direction achieves a very high separation (99.6%) between aligned and misaligned activations at the final layer, suggesting a universal mechanism for detecting and mitigating misalignment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical deployment.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model responses.
The updated engine also delivers faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
This feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: The Atlantic has made four datasets of music used to train AI models publicly searchable.
Two of the discovered music datasets are massive, containing 12 million and 9 million tracks respectively.
This development highlights the transparency and scale of data used in training large-scale AI models.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: The FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of 'agency drama,' notably including a refusal by a Trump official to review the vaccine.
This marks a significant milestone in the regulatory approval of mRNA technology.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for national defense.
This defense spending is also expected to stimulate overseas business growth for Taiwan.
The context of potential threats from China is driving this military hardware expansion.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses the static nature of high-quality flow-matching TTS models, which fail on out-of-vocabulary words post-deployment.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that learns pronunciation corrections via latent conditioning edits instead of full model retraining.
The method optimizes a token-level perturbation within the text embedding space when corrective feedback is supplied.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces StylisticBias, a new controlled benchmark for evaluating attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses the critical gap in understanding how visual cues influence MLLM judgments of people, separating appearance effects from identity differences.
Highlights the growing societal risk associated with deploying MLLMs in consequential settings without understanding their inherent biases.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The paper proposes a new method for LLM alignment that utilizes implicit user feedback instead of relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
Existing LLM alignment methods are limited by the difficulty and cost of collecting high-quality explicit preference annotations from users.
The research aims to leverage implicit signals, similar to those used by major internet companies, to improve LLM alignment efficiency.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The focus is on the text-generation pipeline, indicating a large language model evaluation.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically benefiting users on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes 'local-first' operation, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Hue’s wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Philips Hue introduced wired wall modules to integrate non-smart lighting into its ecosystem.
The new modules are designed to be installed behind existing wall switches.
Hue also launched more affordable versions of its Signe series lamps and updated E14 bulbs.

stories.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
stories.author: Matt Burgess, Maddy Varner, May Bulman, Gabriel Geiger, WIRED.com
bullet_points: The UK plans to use facial scanning technology for age verification of asylum seekers.
Testing has revealed significant risks associated with the accuracy of this age-verification technology.
The deployment proceeds despite documented flaws in the technology's reliability.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service this month.
Significant changes related to this verification are scheduled to begin in September.
The update will address and define support for various app stores on Android.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM that operates significantly in a continuous latent space.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It introduces a novel method, cross-attention attribution, adapting the DAAM framework to the speech domain for the first time.
The technique generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers of speech diffusion models to improve controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current agents where state information (facts, constraints) is implicitly reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses on improving policy-adherence in complex domains like customer service by explicitly tracking state during tool-calling.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: Introduces DeepSWIP, a new method to handle counterfactual reasoning in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
Addresses the limitation of standard associational inference by providing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
Achieves this by using neural materialization to map neural predicates to standard ProbLog choices and applying SWIPs.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: SARLO-80 introduces a new, large-scale dataset for combining Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and optical imagery.
The dataset addresses a critical gap in multimodal AI by providing high-resolution, physically grounded SAR data, unlike existing intensity-only benchmarks.
This resource is crucial for advancing physically grounded multimodal learning models, which currently lack comparable SAR-optical benchmarks.

stories.title: Sovereign Execution Brokers: Enforcing Certificate-Bound Authority in Agentic Control Planes
stories.author: Deying Yu
bullet_points: Addresses the security gap where autonomous agents' mutation authority is not reliably enforced during runtime.
Proposes the Sovereign Execution Broker (SEB) as a mandatory runtime enforcement boundary for certified actions.
Distinguishes itself from existing identity-based access control and assurance layers by providing a concrete enforcement point.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of LiveCodeBench (LCB) to support multiple programming languages.
LCB is an established benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, known for its contamination-aware design.
The research addresses a key limitation of LCB, which was previously restricted solely to Python, aiming to test cross-language generalization in LLMs.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a new, fine-grained privacy framework.
Addresses the limitations of traditional Differential Privacy (DP) by moving beyond worst-case guarantees.
Explicitly incorporates attacker knowledge, compromised data portions, and query families to measure leakage.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, unlike traditional high-throughput systems.
Proposes 'Execution-State Capsules' to manage the full execution state, not just the KV cache, which is crucial for interactive agents.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent branching and tight responsiveness budgets.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation of LLM coding agents: their lack of high-level operational knowledge about a software repository.
The paper investigates the effectiveness of using supplementary context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide coding agents.
The proposed method, 'Probe-and-Refine Tuning,' aims to improve agent performance by providing better repository guidance.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured state for managing complex tasks.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving reliability in customer-service domains by explicitly tracking state across tool calls and policy adherence.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale, bilingual (German/English) dataset of 1.2M CT/MR image-text pairs for radiology.
Presents RefRad2D's creation method, which uses LLM-based curation and automated segmentation to avoid manual spatial annotations.
The resulting model, RadGrounder, is designed to jointly perform report generation, visual question answering, and spatial grounding in medical imaging.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for training counterspeech.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating non-repetitive and effective counterspeech against harmful online content.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-oriented inference optimization techniques crucial for scalable and low-cost large model operations.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
The research provides a systematic review of current key technologies and industry status across these optimization levels.

stories.title: Agency stole bestselling author's book, used AI to relaunch as their own
stories.author: ridesisapis
bullet_points: A book by a bestselling author was allegedly stolen by an agency.
The agency reportedly used AI tools to relaunch the stolen work as their own.
The story highlights ethical concerns regarding AI usage and intellectual property theft.

stories.title: Can AI actually find the root cause of a bug, or does it just sound confident?
stories.author: atharvapanegai
bullet_points: The article discusses the capability of AI to perform automated Root Cause Analysis (RCA) for software bugs.
A tool named 'Pinaka' was developed to automate RCA by analyzing Jira tickets and codebase data.
The core question explored is whether AI provides genuine root cause identification or merely generates confident-sounding summaries.

stories.title: Le dije a un atacante de IA que ganó. Perdió.
stories.author: magopredator
bullet_points: Introduction of 'misdirection-proxy v0.5.0', a new security gateway.
The proxy uses controlled disinformation instead of predictable blocks to defend against AI attackers.
The mechanism aims to degrade the attacker's optimizer until it reaches a Markov collapse.
